_meta.purpose: Runtime validation rules — inputs that fail these produce structured errors, not computations

hard_errors.message: Terminal growth rate (${terminalG}) must be strictly less than WACC (${wacc}). Value would be infinite.
Shares outstanding must be positive. Cannot compute per-share value.
FCFF DCF is not applicable for financial companies (banks, insurers, fintechs). FCF is distorted by loan book flows. Use FCFE instead.
Risk-free rate ${rfRate} is outside plausible bounds [0.001, 0.20]. Check FRED data source.

warnings.message: Terminal growth rate ${terminalG} exceeds 3%. This implies the company will grow faster than the world economy indefinitely.
Residual value is ${pct}% of total EV. Small changes in terminal assumptions have outsized impact. Run sensitivity analysis.
Base FCF is negative for a ${companyType} company. Valuation assumes FCF turns positive within the forecast period.
D/E ratio of ${debtToEquity} is very high. Verify that deposit liabilities (banks) or operating leases are not included as financial debt.
Computed upside/downside of ${pct}% is extreme. Verify FCF normalization and input data quality.
DCF method 1 (${m1}) and method 2 (${m2}) diverge by more than 0.5%. Input inconsistency detected.
Beta of ${beta} is outside typical range [0.3, 3.5]. Regression may have insufficient history or the stock has very low liquidity.

missing_input_policy.rule: If a REQUIRED input is missing, return a structured error — do NOT substitute defaults silently.
